title: Tibet’s Role in China’s Green Energy Supply Chain
gist: Tibet is central to China's green energy supply chain, hosting major solar, wind, and hydropower projects including the planned Metok mega-dam on the Yarlung Tsangpo, plus newly discovered chromite and high-purity quartz deposits that China is racing to exploit to cut import reliance for solar panels and semiconductors.

insights.claim: China, the world leader in renewable manufacturing and processing capacity, is building major green infrastructure in Tibet including large solar projects and the planned Metok (Motuo) mega-dam on the Yarlung Tsangpo.
insights.speaker: Dhondup Wangmo
insights.so_what: Tibet's positioning at the front of China's wind, solar, and hydropower build-out ties the region's development to China's energy transition.
insights.locator: It is building major green infrastructure in Tibet

insights.claim: A newly identified chromite deposit near Amdo in Nagchu Prefecture adds over 1.1 million tons of associated chromite ore, while the Luobusha area in Lhokha holds another 738,300 metric tons of chromite resources in Tibet.
insights.speaker: Dhondup Wangmo
insights.so_what: New chromite finds bolster China's domestic supply of a strategic mineral used in stainless steel and alloys.
insights.locator: Recently, a newly identified chromite deposit

insights.claim: China has identified a new source of high-purity quartz in Tingkye County, Shigatse, aiming to reduce reliance on imports of this critical material used in solar panels and semiconductors.
insights.speaker: Dhondup Wangmo
insights.so_what: Domestic high-purity quartz would ease a supply chokepoint for China's solar and chip industries currently dependent on imports.
insights.locator: China has also identified a new source of high purity quartz
